title: Global markets fall on massive tech sell-off and AI fears
summary: The Nasdaq leads losses with a 2.6% decline as investors reduce risk amid possible rate hikes and cooling enthusiasm for artificial intelligence.

title: SpaceX loses more than $600 billion in market value amid sell-off wave
summary: SpaceX shares plunge up to 16.4% in their worst day since going public, dragged down by valuation doubts and the macroeconomic environment.

title: Oracle cuts 21,000 jobs worldwide in its transition to artificial intelligence
summary: The tech company reduced 13% of its workforce in the past year as part of a restructuring process to focus on the cloud and AI business.

title: European Parliament approves regulation to boost the digital euro
summary: The European Parliament gives the green light to the legal framework for the ECB's digital currency, aiming to ensure European sovereignty in payments and reduce dependence on big tech companies.

title: Bank of America forecasts an additional 15% rise for the Nikkei in 2026
summary: The US bank estimates that the Japanese index has room to keep rising, supported by corporate reforms and the momentum of the tech sector.

title: Carnival cuts quarterly forecasts due to rising costs but improves annual outlook
summary: The cruise company reports a drop in quarterly revenue and cuts estimates for the current quarter, although it raises its adjusted profit guidance for 2026.

title: Chewy shares rise on buy rating and acquisition speculation
summary: The pet retailer gains value after a positive signal from TD Cowen and comments on its potential as a takeover target.

title: Texas Pacific Land surges after agreement with Chevron for electricity supply
summary: The land company signs a deal with Chevron to supply energy to data centers, boosting its stock by more than 4%.

title: AST SpaceMobile rises after announcing satellite launch in August
summary: The satellite telecommunications company gains more than 4% after confirming the launch plan for its constellation next month.

title: US Supreme Court backs Exxon's claim for expropriations in Cuba
summary: The Supreme Court allows Exxon to seek compensation for property confiscated in Cuba after the revolution, in a precedent-setting case.

title: India to divest up to 2% stake in Indian Railway Finance Corp
summary: The Indian government places a minority stake in the railway finance company on the market as part of its privatization plan.

title: iShares Semiconductor ETF plummets in tech sell-off wave
summary: The exchange-traded fund tracking the semiconductor sector falls sharply on a risk-off day with fears over chip demand.

title: Applied Materials sinks alongside the semiconductor sector
summary: Shares of the chip equipment maker fall amid doubts about AI spending and monetary tightening.

title: Gold and silver retreat on rate hike expectations and dollar strengthening
summary: Precious metals weaken amid increased risk aversion and expectations of a more aggressive Fed.

title: Bitcoin and ether fall on tech market sell-off wave
summary: Cryptocurrencies suffer due to increased risk aversion and possible rate hikes, while bitcoin ETFs see outflows.

title: Apollo's private credit fund receives redemption requests of 17% in Q2
summary: Apollo's flagship fund faces an avalanche of redemption requests, exceeding the usual limit, generating uncertainty in the private credit market.

title: Santander surpasses Inditex to become the most valuable company on the Spanish stock exchange
summary: The bank regains the top spot by market capitalization in Spain, supported by its international exposure and the rise in interest rates.

title: Alan Greenspan, former US Federal Reserve chairman, dies
summary: The former head of the Fed for nearly two decades dies at 98, leaving a controversial legacy in US monetary policy.

title: Intel CEO warns chipmaker's recovery will take longer
summary: Patrick Gelsinger issues a cautious message on business prospects, citing weaker demand and intense competition.

title: Chevron partners with Microsoft to supply electricity to AI data centers
summary: The US oil company enters the electricity generation business to meet the growing demand from Microsoft's artificial intelligence data centers.
